# Building Microservices, Second Edition (Sam Newman)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [What Are Microservices?](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/01-what-are-microservices.md): Designing, refactoring, or reviewing distributed systems and microservice architectures, including defining service boundaries, API contracts, and inter-process communication; migrating or decomposing monolithic applications; configuring databases, logging, monitoring, and deployment topologies; advising on organizational structures and team topologies; and evaluating architectural pattern trade-offs.
    - [How to Model Microservices](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/02-how-to-model-microservices.md): Architectural design, analyzing or defining microservice boundaries, refactoring monolithic applications into distributed systems, evaluating coupling and cohesion between system components, modeling business domains using Domain-Driven Design (DDD), or designing inter-process communication contracts and data schemas.
    - [Splitting the Monolith](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/03-splitting-the-monolith.md): Architectural design, code refactoring, or infrastructure planning involving the decomposition of a monolithic application into microservices, including tasks such as splitting databases, separating application layers, managing legacy system migrations, implementing routing proxies, and handling data synchronization in distributed architectures.
    - [Microservice Communication Styles](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/04-microservice-communication-styles.md): Designing, implementing, refactoring, or reviewing communication between microservices, defining APIs, selecting integration technologies (e.g., HTTP, gRPC, Message Brokers), handling distributed system errors, defining event schemas, or optimizing inter-process network calls.
    - [Implementing Microservice Communication](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/05-implementing-microservice-communication.md): Designing, implementing, refactoring, or documenting communication between microservices, defining API contracts (REST, gRPC, GraphQL), configuring asynchronous message brokers, managing API versioning/schemas, implementing service discovery, or configuring API gateways and service meshes.
    - [Workflow](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/06-workflow.md): Designing, implementing, evaluating, or refactoring business processes, state changes, or data modifications that span multiple microservices, distinct database boundaries, or distributed systems.
    - [Build](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/07-build.md): Generating, modifying, or reviewing CI/CD pipelines, source control configurations, branching strategies, repository structures, artifact generation scripts (e.g., Dockerfiles, build.sh), or release automation workflows in a microservices environment.
    - [Deployment](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/08-deployment.md): The deployment, provisioning, physical architecture design, or operational configuration of a microservice architecture.
    - [Testing](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/09-testing.md): Writing test code, configuring CI/CD test pipelines, designing testing architectures, writing test doubles (stubs/mocks), or refactoring existing test suites for microservice-based applications.
    - [From Monitoring to Observability](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/10-from-monitoring-to-observability.md): Designing, implementing, configuring, instrumenting, or troubleshooting telemetry, logging, metrics, distributed tracing, alerting, or general observability strategies within a microservice or distributed systems architecture.
    - [Security](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/11-security.md): Designing, implementing, configuring, or reviewing the security architecture of microservices, including tasks involving authentication, authorization, secrets management, network security, data encryption (at rest and in transit), continuous integration/delivery security, identity providers (IdP), and inter-service communication protocols.
    - [Resiliency](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/12-resiliency.md): Distributed systems resiliency and fault tolerance, encompassing microservice reliability, high availability, stability patterns (Circuit Breakers, Bulkheads, Retries, Timeouts), CAP Theorem trade-offs, and proactive failure engineering such as Chaos Engineering.
    - [Scaling](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/13-scaling.md): Scaling systems, handling increased architectural load, improving system performance or latency, implementing caching strategies, designing database partitioning (sharding), adding read replicas, configuring autoscaling, or evaluating the Command Query Responsibility Segregation (CQRS) and Event Sourcing patterns.
    - [User Interfaces](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/14-user-interfaces.md): Designing, structuring, refactoring, or integrating User Interfaces (UIs) in a microservice architecture, encompassing frontend-to-backend communication, frontend decomposition, Single-Page Application (SPA) modularization, API Gateway configuration, Backend for Frontend (BFF) implementation, and GraphQL schema design for client consumption.
    - [Organizational Structures](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/15-organizational-structures.md): Designing or modifying microservice boundaries, configuring repository ownership, generating organizational documentation, establishing continuous integration/code review workflows, scaffolding platform or enablement tools, or evaluating system architectures for scaling development teams, with a specific focus on aligning technical systems with organizational communication structures (Conway's Law).
    - [The Evolutionary Architect](.roo/vendor/architecture/building-microservices-second-edition-sam-newman/16-the-evolutionary-architect.md): System architecture design, microservice boundary definition, cross-service communication planning, technical governance, implementing "paved road" templates, defining fitness functions, or making trade-offs regarding technical debt and system evolution.
